nodes.title: Validate constructor input first
nodes.why: `new`-style construction only works with callable constructors, so this fails early with a clear message.
nodes.alternative: Skip the guard and let `apply` throw later.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more code, but much clearer failure behavior.

nodes.title: Create prototype-linked instance, then call constructor
nodes.why: This mirrors how native `new` works: set prototype first, then run the constructor with that object as `this`.
nodes.tradeoff: Correct `instanceof` and prototype behavior with minimal extra complexity.

nodes.title: Respect constructor return override rule

anchor.snippet: const isObject = result !== null && (typeof result === 'object' || typeof result === 'function');

nodes.why: If constructor returns an object/function, native `new` returns that; otherwise it returns the created instance.
nodes.tradeoff: More accurate native behavior, at the cost of one explicit type check.
